title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to AlwaysPullImages forces every new pod to pull the required images every time. In a multi-tenant cluster users can be assured that their private images can only be used by those who have the credentials to pull them. Without this admission control policy, once an image has been pulled to a node, any pod from any user can use it simply by knowing the image’s name, without any authorization check against the image ownership. When this plug-in is enabled, images are always pulled prior to starting containers, which means valid credentials are required. Impact: Credentials would be required to pull the private images every time. Also, in trusted environments, this might increases load on network, registry, and decreases speed. This setting could impact offline or isolated clusters, which have images preloaded and do not have access to a registry to pull in-use images. This setting is not appropriate for clusters which use this configuration.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes AlwaysPullImages.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --enable-admission- plugins parameter to include AlwaysPullImages. --enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,... Default Value: By default, AlwaysPullImages is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/admission- controllers/#alwayspullimages
input_row.impact: Credentials would be required to pull the private images every time. Also, in trusted environments, this might increases load on network, registry, and decreases speed. This setting could impact offline or isolated clusters, which have images preloaded and do not have access to a registry to pull in-use images. This setting is not appropriate for clusters which use this configuration.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugin_always_pull_images_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to verify that the `AlwaysPullImages` admission control plugin is enabled.
- If using static pods (common in kubeadm and managed clusters), inspect the manifest file at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` for the `--enable-admission-plugins` flag.
- Alternatively, if the API server is managed differently, retrieve the running process arguments for `kube-apiserver` and check for the presence of `--enable-admission-plugins=...AlwaysPullImages...`.
- **Automated Steps:**
  1. Use `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` to find the pod spec and check the `command` or `args` field for `--enable-admission-plugins`.
  2. Alternatively, on the control plane node, parse `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` for the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument.
  3. Confirm that `AlwaysPullImages` is present in the comma-separated list of enabled admission plugins.
- **Pass Criteria:** The `--enable-admission-plugins` flag includes `AlwaysPullImages` in its value.
- **Fail Criteria:** The `--enable-admission-plugins` flag is missing, or `AlwaysPullImages` is not present in the list.
- **Example CLI Command:**
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath="{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}" | grep -- '--enable-admission-plugins'
  ```
  Or, for static pod manifest:
  ```
  grep -- '--enable-admission-plugins' /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
  ```
  Then verify `AlwaysPullImages` is included in the argument value.
